case_title: ROGER S. CHAN MD VS. MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF CONSUMER
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

docket_entries.description: (1ST APPEARANCE) RESPONDENT MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA'S MEMO OF P&A IN OPPOS TO PETITIONER'S MEMORANDUM ISO PETITION FOR PEREMPTORY OR ALTERNATIVE WRIT OF MANDATE FILED BY RESPONDENT MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT FILED BY RESPONDENT

PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION FILED BY PETITIONER CHAN MD, ROGER S. AS TO RESPONDENT MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF CONSUMER AFFAIRS STATE OF CALIFORNIA JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: NO ARGUMENT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER ROGER CHAN'S HEARING ON WRIT OF MANDAMUS IS DENIED. THE COURT DENIES PETITIONER'S REQUEST TO INCLUDE THE JULY 26, 2013, CLARIFICATION OF PACE PHYSICIAN ASSESSMENT OF ROGER CHAN, M.D ("CLARIFICATION") DOCUMENT TO THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD. (PETITIONER'S EXHIBITS TO PETITION FOR ALTERNATIVE WRIT OF MANDATE, EXH. 11-C.) PETITIONER HAS NOT SHOWN THAT HE EXERCISED REASONABLE DILIGENCE IN ATTEMPTING TO PRODUCE THE CLARIFICATION DOCUMENT AT THE ADMINISTRATIVE HEARING. (CCP 1094.5(E).) EVEN IF THE COURT CONSIDERED THE CLARIFICATION DOCUMENT, UPON INDEPENDENT REVIEW, THE WEIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE SUPPORTS THE ADMINISTRATIVE LAW JUDGE'S FINDINGS. A STRONG PRESUMPTION OF CORRECTNESS IS AFFORDED TO THE ADMINISTRATIVE LAW JUDGE'S FINDINGS. (FUKUDA V. CITY OF ANGELS (1999) 20 CAL.4TH 805, 817.) THE EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT RESPONDENT HAD LEGAL GROUNDS TO DISCIPLINE PETITIONER UNDER BUSINESS & PROFESSIONS CODE 2305. (ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD, EXH. 1.) FURTHER, THE WEIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE SUPPORTS THE FINDING THAT RESPONDENT'S DISCIPLINARY REQUIREMENTS ARE CONSISTENT WITH ITS GOALS TO "AID IN THE REHABILITATION OF THE LICENSEE." (BUSINESS & PROFESSIONS CODE 2229.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976
THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: - JUDGMENT ON ORDER DENYING PETITION FOR PEREMPTORY OR ALTERNATIVE WRIT OF MANDATE - SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion Of Hearing On Writ Of Mandamus
Off Calendar Jan-30-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ROBERT STEWART, ESQ., 415.479.3600, FOR PETITIONER ROGER S. CHAN MD.
BRENDA REYES, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, 415.703.5541, FOR RESPONDENT MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF CONSUMER AFFAIRS.

RULING: No argument. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER ROGER CHAN'S Hearing On Writ Of Mandamus is denied. The Court denies Petitioner's request to include the July 26, 2013, Clarification of PACE Physician Assessment of Roger Chan, M.D ("Clarification") document to the Administrative Record. (Petitioner's Exhibits to Petition for Alternative Writ of Mandate, Exh. 11-C.) Petitioner has not shown that he exercised reasonable diligence in attempting to produce the Clarification document at the administrative hearing. (CCP 1094.5(e).) Even if the Court considered the Clarification document, upon independent review, the weight of the evidence supports the Administrative Law Judge's findings. A strong presumption of correctness is afforded to the Administrative Law Judge's findings. (Fukuda v. City of Angels (1999) 20 Cal.4th 805, 817.) The evidence shows that Respondent had legal grounds to discipline Petitioner under Business & Professions Code 2305. (Administrative Record, Exh. 1.) Further, the weight of the evidence supports the finding that Respondent's disciplinary requirements are consistent with its goals to "aid in the rehabilitation of the licensee." (Business & Professions Code 2229.) Order signed in open court. Judgment signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976
